title: A KdV conservative update preserves total mass when boundary fluxes match

source.notes: Boundary-equality version of KdV conservative total preservation.

informal.statement: If the KdV-like flux at the right boundary equals the flux at the left boundary, then the KdV conservative update preserves the total over the finite range.
assumptions: scale, nonlinear, and dispersion are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state
n is the number of cells in the finite range
the right and left KdV fluxes are equal
informal.expected_conclusion: The KdV conservative update preserves the finite total.

declarations.name: totalOn_kdvConservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_kdvConservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq (scale nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u n = kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u 0) : totalOn (fun i => kdvConservativeUpdate scale nonlinear dispersion u i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Translate KdV total preservation under matching boundary fluxes into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem totalOn_kdvConservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq (scale nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u n = kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u 0) : totalOn (fun i => kdvConservativeUpdate scale nonlinear dispersion u i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Given the generic boundary-equality conservative update theorem, complete the KdV proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding kdvConservativeUpdate and applying totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq.
